title: Document that `force_mask` affects UID and GID too
body:
Edit: See my comment below. This has already been implemented, but it is not documented yet.

The `force_mask` option allows us to control file permissions within a container, independently of the permissions outside the container, by storing the permissions used within the container in an extended attribute.

A similar mechanism for file ownership would be useful. This would ensure that, from outside the container, all files in the storage location are owned by the same user ID, which would positively impact at least the following use cases:

- Tools such as backup applications that don't support entering user namespaces and therefore have trouble accessing file contents.
- It would also make changing user ID and subuid ranges much easier since all the files in the storage location could simply be chowned to the new user ID.
- It would support the use of dynamic subuid and subgid ranges, which are necessary for systemd-nsresourced to work (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24828).

comments.body:
I just found out that this feature seems to already be covered by the `force_mask` option. When this option is used, the extended attribute `user.containers.override_stat` contains values in the format `uid:gid:mode`, and the actual UID and GID on the host are independent.

This doesn't seem to be documented yet, so this issue is a documentation issue.
would you like to open a PR to add the missing bits?
